headline: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.

content.title: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
content.summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.

headline: Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

content.title: Iran ceasefire market moves: bitcoin prices surge, Exxon stock falls
content.description:
Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.

Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.

Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.
content.summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Brooke DiPalma take a look at some of Wednesday morning's trending tickers and stories.Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices have surged to a three-week high on news of a ceasefire with Iran. The cryptocurrency is also rising after Iran requested that toll payments be made in crypto.Energy stocks, including Exxon (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Occidental (OXY), are falling from the ceasefire news.Cruise stocks, including Norwegian (NCLH), Royal Caribbean (RCL), and Carnival (CCL), are soaring on eased US–Iran tensions.

headline: Carnival Pops 10%, Norwegian Rises 7%: Six Days After Falling on Fuel Fears, Cruise Stocks Are Surging

headline: Delta Stock Soars After Earnings. These Airlines Are Climbing Even Higher.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock jumped on Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S.-Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Delta advanced 5.4% to $69.16, putting the stock on pace for its highest close since Feb. 26, when it ended the session at $70.51.  Airline stocks, too, were rising sharply, with  Southwest Airlines  up 6.5% and  United Airlines  spiking 9.3%.

content.title: Delta Stock Soars After Earnings. These Airlines Are Climbing Even Higher.
content.summary: Delta Air Lines  stock jumped on Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S.-Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Delta advanced 5.4% to $69.16, putting the stock on pace for its highest close since Feb. 26, when it ended the session at $70.51.  Airline stocks, too, were rising sharply, with  Southwest Airlines  up 6.5% and  United Airlines  spiking 9.3%.

headline: THE MOST REWARDING LOYALTY PROGRAM IN LUXURY CRUISING: SILVERSEA ENHANCES ITS VENETIAN SOCIETY WITH NEW LOYALTY TIERS AND ENHANCED BENEFITS
summary: Silversea, the leading experiential luxury and expedition travel brand, is enhancing its Venetian Society loyalty program, offering guests more ways to earn status, earlier recognition, and meaningful benefits throughout their journey – becoming the richest loyalty program among any luxury cruise brand. Guided by guest feedback, the enhanced program will debut on July 1, 2026, with two new loyalty milestones and enriching benefits across every tier.

content.title: THE MOST REWARDING LOYALTY PROGRAM IN LUXURY CRUISING: SILVERSEA ENHANCES ITS VENETIAN SOCIETY WITH NEW LOYALTY TIERS AND ENHANCED BENEFITS

Guided by guest feedback, the enhanced program will debut on July 1, 2026, with two new loyalty milestones and enriching benefits across every tier.

thumbnail.caption: Silversea enhances its Venetian Society with new loyalty tiers and enhanced benefits.

headline: Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) Valuation Check After Launch Of Royal ONE Tri Branded Credit Cards
summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) is drawing fresh attention after partnering with Bank of America on the Royal ONE and Royal ONE Plus Visa Signature credit cards, the cruise industry’s first tri-branded rewards program. See our latest analysis for Royal Caribbean Cruises. The Royal ONE card launch comes as momentum has cooled in recent months, with a 90-day share price return decline of 8.24% and a 30-day share price return decline of 3.89%, even though the 1-year total shareholder return is...
